varieties.name: Crowley
varieties.origin: Modern selection
varieties.growing_notes: Modern commercial variety. Cold climate zones 4-7.

varieties.name: Wentworth
varieties.origin: Modern selection

varieties.name: Redwing
varieties.origin: Modern selection

varieties.name: Alfredo Compact
varieties.origin: Compact variety selection
varieties.traits: Compact growth habit, suitable for smaller spaces

varieties.name: Baily Compact
varieties.origin: Compact variety selection
varieties.traits: Compact growth habit

varieties.name: Compactum
varieties.origin: Compact variety selection
varieties.traits: Dwarf habit, smaller growth
varieties.growing_notes: Suitable for container growing in cold climates. Zones 4-7.

varieties.name: Vaccinium oxycoccos (Small/Bog/European Cranberry)
varieties.origin: Circumpolar native species - Northern/Central Europe, northern Asia, Canada, northern USA
varieties.traits: Smaller plant than V. macrocarpon, small oval leaves with acute tips, margins roll under, trailing vine habit, smaller berries (0.5-1cm), whitish leaf undersides
varieties.flesh_fruit: Smaller, drier berries than American cranberry, very tart, edible, rich in proanthocyanins and anthocyanins
varieties.yield_notes: Lower yields than American cranberry, berries persist through winter under snow
varieties.growing_notes: Cultivated in Central/Northern Europe (Finland, Germany, Lithuania). Rarely cultivated in North America. Used by Indigenous peoples (Iñupiat, Dena'ina, Woods Cree, Menomini). Can spread by layering. Requires cold bog/fen conditions. Synonyms: Oxycoccus oxycoccos, O. palustris, O. quadripetalus. Cold zones 2-7.
varieties.availability: European nurseries, rarely available in North America except wild harvesting

varieties.name: Vaccinium oxycoccos var. microcarpus (Little-leaf Cranberry)
varieties.origin: Variety of European cranberry, northern climates
varieties.traits: Even smaller leaves and berries than species
varieties.growing_notes: Found in subarctic regions. Extremely cold hardy. Wild harvest only.

varieties.name: Roselle / Florida Cranberry (Hibiscus sabdariffa) 🌱
varieties.origin: Central/West Africa, widely naturalized in tropics/subtropics worldwide
varieties.traits: Shrubby annual/perennial (4-8 feet tall), lobed dark green leaves, yellow flowers with red center, edible red calyces
varieties.flesh_fruit: Bright red fleshy calyces (seed pods), tart cranberry-like flavor, rich in vitamin C, antioxidants, anthocyanins
varieties.yield_notes: Abundant calyx production October-December, multiple harvests possible
varieties.growing_notes: PERFECT FOR humid subtropical climates SUBTROPICAL CLIMATE. Plant April-May after frost. Thrives in heat/humidity. Calyces used exactly like cranberries - sauces, jams, teas, juice, fresh cooking. Called 'Florida Cranberry' due to color/flavor similarity. Traditional Caribbean Christmas drink (Sorrel). Also called Rosella, Jamaica Sorrel, Red Sorrel, Indian Sorrel. Leaves also edible (tangy, like spinach). Frost-sensitive. Blooms October, fruits November-December. Non-GMO.
varieties.availability: Readily available humid subtropical climates garden centers, online Australian seed suppliers, subtropical nurseries

varieties.name: Hibiscus sabdariffa 'Victor'
varieties.origin: Selected variety, proven for hot climates
varieties.traits: Variety specifically selected for South Florida/subtropical performance
varieties.growing_notes: Recommended for Queensland climate. Superior performance in humid subtropical climates conditions. Available from tropical/subtropical specialty nurseries.
varieties.availability: Specialty subtropical nurseries, rare seeds Australia

varieties.name: Cranberry Hibiscus (Hibiscus acetosella)
varieties.origin: Africa, tropical/subtropical regions
varieties.traits: Deep cranberry-red leaves (ornamental), 3-6 feet tall, pink hibiscus flowers, lobed maple-like leaves
varieties.flesh_fruit: Leaves are edible (tangy, NOT calyces), ornamental value
varieties.growing_notes: EXCELLENT FOR humid subtropical climates. Grown primarily for edible leaves and ornamental value, not fruit. Called 'false roselle' or 'maroon mallow'. Hardy in subtropics/tropics. Heat/humidity tolerant. Leaves used in salads, cooking, tea. Different from Roselle - this one grown for leaves, Roselle for calyces. Non-GMO.
varieties.availability: humid subtropical climates garden centers, Queensland nurseries, subtropical plant specialists

varieties.name: Native Cranberry / Cranberry Heath (Astroloma humifusum)
varieties.origin: Native to Australia (NSW, ACT, Vic, Tas, SA, WA)
varieties.traits: Mat-forming shrub (<0.5m), prickly narrow leaves, bright red tubular flowers
varieties.growing_notes: Native Australian alternative. NOT a true cranberry (different genus). Tolerates frost and dry periods. Requires well-drained soil. Not specifically suited to humid subtropical climates humidity but grows in NSW ranges. Difficult to propagate.
varieties.availability: Rare - native plant specialists, bushfoods nurseries

varieties.name: Roselle / Florida Cranberry (Hibiscus sabdariffa) 🌱
varieties.origin: Central/West Africa, widely grown in tropical India
varieties.traits: Shrubby annual/perennial (2-2.5m tall in tropical monsoon climates climate), lobed leaves, yellow flowers with red center, bright red calyces
varieties.flesh_fruit: Red fleshy calyces, very tart/tangy flavor similar to cranberry, rich in vitamin C and anthocyanins
varieties.yield_notes: Abundant production, multiple harvests during monsoon season
varieties.growing_notes: PERFECT FOR tropical monsoon climates TROPICAL MONSOON CLIMATE. Thrives in heat, humidity, and monsoon rains. Traditional crop in tropical monsoon climates - widely grown for 'gongura' leaves and calyces. Calyces used exactly like cranberries - chutneys, pickles, drinks, preserves. Leaves used in curries, rice dishes (pulihora). Heat-loving (grows best 25-35°C). Plant after monsoon begins. Highly productive in tropical monsoon climates conditions. Called 'Indian Sorrel' or 'Gongura'. Non-GMO, open-pollinated.
varieties.availability: Widely available - tropical monsoon climates nurseries, agricultural stores, local markets (tropical monsoon climates, Pathanamthitta, Kottayam), seed suppliers across India

varieties.name: Roselle - Green Calyx Variety
varieties.origin: Selected Indian variety
varieties.traits: Green calyces instead of red, same plant growth

varieties.name: Cranberry Hibiscus (Hibiscus acetosella)
varieties.origin: Africa, tropical regions
varieties.traits: Deep red/maroon leaves, 1-2m tall, ornamental and edible
varieties.flesh_fruit: Edible leaves (tangy flavor), used fresh or cooked
varieties.growing_notes: EXCELLENT FOR tropical monsoon climates. Thrives in monsoon humidity and heat. Grown for edible ornamental leaves, not fruit. Used in tropical monsoon climates kitchen gardens. Leaves used in salads, chutneys, as spinach substitute. Highly productive year-round. Heat/humidity tolerant (optimal for tropical monsoon climates). Also called 'false roselle'. Non-GMO.
varieties.availability: tropical monsoon climates nurseries (tropical monsoon climates area), tropical plant specialists, online Indian nurseries

varieties.name: Karonda / Carissa (Carissa carandas) 🌏
varieties.origin: Native to India
varieties.traits: Thorny shrub, 2-4m tall, small white flowers, clusters of berries
varieties.flesh_fruit: Small round berries (1-2cm), green to black when ripe, very tart/astringent when unripe, sweet-tart when fully ripe

varieties.name: Jamun / Java Plum (Syzygium cumini) 🌏
varieties.origin: Native to India, Southeast Asia
varieties.traits: Large evergreen tree (10-15m), dark purple-black berries
varieties.flesh_fruit: Oblong berries, astringent-sweet, purple flesh, staining

varieties.name: Bilimbi / Tree Sorrel (Averrhoa bilimbi) 🌏
varieties.origin: Native to Indonesia, naturalized in tropical monsoon climates
varieties.traits: Small tree (5-10m), compound leaves, clusters of yellowish-green fruits on trunk/branches

varieties.name: Kokum (Garcinia indica) 🌏
varieties.origin: Native to Western Ghats (tropical monsoon climates, Karnataka, Goa, Maharashtra)
varieties.traits: Evergreen tree (10-15m), dark purple-red fruits
varieties.flesh_fruit: Round fruits (2-4cm), dried outer rind used, very sour, cooling properties

varieties.name: Passion Fruit - Purple (Passiflora edulis)
varieties.origin: South America, widely cultivated tropical monsoon climates
varieties.traits: Vigorous vine, purple fruits (golf ball size)
varieties.flesh_fruit: Purple-skinned, aromatic yellow pulp, tart-sweet flavor, seeds edible
varieties.yield_notes: High productivity, year-round with peak monsoon
varieties.growing_notes: SUCCESSFULLY CULTIVATED IN tropical monsoon climates (Pathanamthitta, Idukki districts). Thrives in monsoon humidity. Tart flavor profile. Used in juices, desserts, preserves. Commercially grown in tropical monsoon climates hill stations and midlands. Productive in tropical conditions. Non-GMO.
varieties.availability: tropical monsoon climates nurseries (tropical monsoon climates, Pathanamthitta), tropical monsoon climates agricultural department, specialty fruit nurseries

varieties.name: Gooseberry / Amla (Phyllanthus emblica) 🌏
varieties.origin: Native to India
varieties.traits: Medium tree (8-15m), light green fruits in clusters
varieties.flesh_fruit: Round berries (2-3cm), extremely sour/astringent, fibrous, becomes sweet after initial sourness

varieties.name: Tamarind (Tamarindus indica) 🌏
varieties.origin: Tropical Africa, naturalized India for millennia
varieties.traits: Large evergreen tree (20-25m), curved brown pods
varieties.flesh_fruit: Brown sticky pulp inside pods, very sour-sweet, concentrated tartness

varieties.name: Rambuttan (Nephelium lappaceum)
varieties.origin: Southeast Asia, successfully cultivated tropical monsoon climates (Pathanamthitta district)
varieties.traits: Medium tree (12-20m), hairy red fruits in clusters
varieties.flesh_fruit: White translucent flesh, sweet with slight tartness, grape-like texture
varieties.growing_notes: SUCCESSFULLY NATURALIZED IN tropical monsoon climates, especially Pathanamthitta district (very near tropical monsoon climates). Thrives in monsoon climate. Sweet-tart flavor. Commercially cultivated. Related to lychee. Productive in tropical conditions.
varieties.availability: tropical monsoon climates nurseries (particularly Pathanamthitta district nurseries, very local to tropical monsoon climates area)

varieties.name: Mangoosteen (Garcinia mangostana)
varieties.origin: Southeast Asia, cultivated tropical monsoon climates
varieties.traits: Evergreen tree (10-25m), purple round fruits
varieties.flesh_fruit: White segmented flesh, slightly sweet-tart, aromatic
varieties.growing_notes: Grown in tropical monsoon climates (Pathanamthitta, Ernakulam). Thrives in tropical monsoon. 'Queen of fruits'. Sweet with slight tartness. High-value exotic fruit in tropical monsoon climates.
varieties.availability: Specialty tropical monsoon climates nurseries, exotic fruit growers Pathanamthitta/Idukki
